ghosts.definition: spirits of dead people that some believe can appear to the living
ghosts.example: The child was afraid of ghosts in the dark room.

monsters.example: He said there were monsters under his bed.

dismissing.definition: treating something as not important or not worth thinking about
dismissing.example: Dismissing her fear did not help her feel better.

vanquish.example: Together they made up a story to vanquish the monsters.

soothed.definition: made to feel calm and less upset
soothed.example: The child felt soothed after his mother held him.

silly.definition: not serious, foolish, not worth worrying about
silly.example: She told him not to be silly about the dark.

impatience.definition: the feeling of not wanting to wait, being annoyed by delay
impatience.example: His impatience at bedtime made the child feel rushed.

bedtime.definition: the time when you go to sleep
bedtime.example: The children had a set bedtime every night.

complicated.definition: having many parts, hard to understand
complicated.example: Feelings can be complicated, even for adults.

articulate.definition: to express a thought or feeling clearly in words
articulate.example: A young child may not be able to articulate their fears.

trace.definition: to find the beginning or cause of something
trace.example: It was hard to trace where his fear started.

source.definition: the place where something begins or comes from
source.example: We could not find the source of her worry.

communication.definition: sharing thoughts or feelings with another person, by talking or other ways
communication.example: Good communication between parents and children is important.

distinction.definition: a clear difference between two things
distinction.example: The distinction between safe and dangerous is clear to adults.

assume.definition: to believe something is true without checking
assume.example: We should not assume children see things the same way we do.

creepy.definition: making you feel scared or uncomfortable in a strange way
creepy.example: The creepy house made the children afraid to go inside.

lentil.definition: a small, round seed that is cooked and eaten as food
lentil.example: Granny made a warm lentil stew for dinner.

stew.definition: a hot dish made by cooking meat or vegetables slowly in liquid
stew.example: The stew was thick and full of vegetables.

icky.definition: something that feels bad or disgusting (informal word)
icky.example: The child said the food tasted icky.

irrelevant.definition: not connected to what is being discussed, not important
irrelevant.example: He thought her complaint was irrelevant.

humiliation.definition: a very strong feeling of shame or embarrassment
humiliation.example: The humiliation of being laughed at stopped her from sharing.

extreme.definition: very great in degree, much more than normal
extreme.example: This may seem like an extreme example.

register.definition: to notice or become aware of something
register.example: The child did not register the danger in the same way.

alarm.definition: a feeling of fear or worry that something bad might happen
alarm.example: She felt alarm when she heard the strange noise.

inappropriately.definition: in a way that is not right or suitable for the situation
inappropriately.example: Being touched inappropriately is never okay.

assault.definition: a sudden attack on someone's body or feelings
assault.example: Both experiences felt like an assault on her senses.

senses.definition: the ways your body takes in the world: sight, hearing, touch, taste, smell
senses.example: The loud noise was an assault on their senses.

sexuality.definition: things related to physical attraction and the body
sexuality.example: A young child has not yet understood sexuality.

representing.definition: standing for or meaning something else
representing.example: The monsters might be representing a deeper fear.

complain.definition: to say that something is wrong or that you are not happy about it
complain.example: The child wanted to complain but was afraid of being laughed at.

filed.definition: put into a group or category in your mind
filed.example: Both feelings were filed under something icky in the child's mind.

clam.definition: to become quiet and stop talking (clam up)
clam.example: If you laugh at children, they will clam up.

dangerous.definition: able to cause harm or hurt someone
dangerous.example: Closing down communication can be a dangerous thing.
